sentence: Last updated: February 23, 2026 | Effective date: February 23, 2026 | Version 2.0

Wizar Learning Inc. ("Wizar," "we," "us," or "our") is a Canadian-incorporated educational technology company headquartered in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada. We operate the Learnverse platform and related services (collectively, the "Platform" or "Service").
Data Controller: Wizar Learning Inc., Vancouver, BC, Canada.
Contact: privacy@wizar.io

This Privacy Policy applies to all users of the Learnverse platform, including students, parents and guardians, teachers, school administrators, and any other individuals who access or use our Service through web browsers, mobile applications, or any other means. It covers data collected through the Platform, our website (wizar.io), and any associated services.
We collect personal data — meaning information that can identify an individual — in the following categories:
| Category | Data Types | Source |
| Account Information | Name, email address, role (student/teacher/parent/admin), school or organisation affiliation, grade level | Provided by user or school administrator at registration |
| Authentication Data | Hashed passwords, single sign-on tokens | Provided at login |
| Learning Data | Progress through curriculum modules, assessment scores, competency levels, quest completion, time on task, portfolio artifacts | Generated through Platform use |
| Communication Data | Messages sent through Platform support channels, survey responses, feedback | Provided by user |
| Parent/Guardian Data | Name and email of parent/guardian (for children under 13) | Provided during parental consent process |
We do not collect: biometric data, precise geolocation, financial information from students, social media profiles of students, or any data not necessary for the educational purpose of the Platform.

section: H2:4. Why We Collect Personal Data (Purpose)
sentence: Every piece of personal data we collect serves a specific educational purpose: To provide the Service: Creating accounts, authenticating users, delivering personalised learning paths, and tracking progress through the AI literacy curriculum.

section: H2:4. Why We Collect Personal Data (Purpose)
sentence: Every piece of personal data we collect serves a specific educational purpose: To support educators: Providing teachers and administrators with dashboards, analytics, and portfolio reports to monitor student learning and inform instruction.

section: H2:4. Why We Collect Personal Data (Purpose)
sentence: Every piece of personal data we collect serves a specific educational purpose: To improve learning outcomes: Analysing aggregated and anonymised learning data to improve curriculum design, identify effective pedagogies, and enhance Platform features.

section: H2:4. Why We Collect Personal Data (Purpose)
sentence: Every piece of personal data we collect serves a specific educational purpose: To communicate: Sending essential service notifications (e.g., password resets, account updates, privacy policy changes) and, with consent, educational updates.

section: H2:4. Why We Collect Personal Data (Purpose)
sentence: Every piece of personal data we collect serves a specific educational purpose: To ensure safety: Detecting and preventing unauthorised access, abuse, or misuse of the Platform.

section: H2:4. Why We Collect Personal Data (Purpose)
sentence: Every piece of personal data we collect serves a specific educational purpose: To comply with legal obligations: Meeting regulatory requirements under applicable privacy laws.

section: H2:5. How We Use Personal Data
sentence: Personal data is used solely to fulfil the purposes stated above. Specifically: Learning data powers adaptive content delivery, formative assessments, and competency tracking within the Platform.

section: H2:5. How We Use Personal Data
sentence: Personal data is used solely to fulfil the purposes stated above. Specifically: Account data is used for authentication, role-based access, and connecting students to their teachers and schools.

section: H2:5. How We Use Personal Data
sentence: Personal data is used solely to fulfil the purposes stated above. Specifically: Aggregated, de-identified data may be used for research to improve educational outcomes. Such data cannot be traced back to any individual.

section: H2:5. How We Use Personal Data
sentence: We never use personal data for advertising, marketing, profiling for commercial purposes, or sale to third parties.
We also collect non-personal (anonymous or aggregated) data that cannot identify any individual: Device and browser information: Device type, operating system, browser type, screen resolution.

We also collect non-personal (anonymous or aggregated) data that cannot identify any individual: Usage analytics: Pages visited, features used, session duration, interaction patterns (clicks, scrolls).
We also collect non-personal (anonymous or aggregated) data that cannot identify any individual: Performance data: Page load times, error logs, crash reports.

We also collect non-personal (anonymous or aggregated) data that cannot identify any individual: Aggregated learning statistics: Class-level or school-level performance metrics, curriculum completion rates, feature engagement patterns.

section: H2:7. How We Use Non-Personal Data
sentence: Non-personal data is used to: Monitor and improve Platform performance, stability, and user experience.

section: H2:7. How We Use Non-Personal Data
sentence: Non-personal data is used to: Understand how features are used and prioritise product development.

section: H2:7. How We Use Non-Personal Data
sentence: Non-personal data is used to: Generate aggregated reports for schools and partners on overall usage and impact.

section: H2:7. How We Use Non-Personal Data
sentence: Non-personal data is used to: Conduct educational research using de-identified data to advance the field of AI literacy education.

section: H2:7. How We Use Non-Personal Data
sentence: Non-personal data is never sold to third parties or used for advertising.

Protecting children is at the core of everything we do. Learnverse is designed for learners in Grades 3–8 (approximately ages 8–13), and we take the following measures: Parental/guardian consent: For children under the age of 13 (or the applicable age of consent in the child's jurisdiction), we require verifiable parental or guardian consent before collecting any personal data, unless the account is created and managed by the child's school under an educational agreement.

Protecting children is at the core of everything we do. Learnverse is designed for learners in Grades 3–8 (approximately ages 8–13), and we take the following measures: School-managed accounts: When schools create student accounts, the school acts as the agent of the parent and provides consent on behalf of families, as permitted under COPPA and equivalent regulations.

Protecting children is at the core of everything we do. Learnverse is designed for learners in Grades 3–8 (approximately ages 8–13), and we take the following measures: No marketing to children: Children's data is never used for marketing, advertising, or promotional purposes — by us or by any third party.

Protecting children is at the core of everything we do. Learnverse is designed for learners in Grades 3–8 (approximately ages 8–13), and we take the following measures: No behavioural targeting: We do not build behavioural profiles of children for any non-educational purpose.

Protecting children is at the core of everything we do. Learnverse is designed for learners in Grades 3–8 (approximately ages 8–13), and we take the following measures: No external advertisements: The Platform does not display any third-party advertisements.

Protecting children is at the core of everything we do. Learnverse is designed for learners in Grades 3–8 (approximately ages 8–13), and we take the following measures: Minimal data collection: We collect only the data necessary to provide the educational service.

Protecting children is at the core of everything we do. Learnverse is designed for learners in Grades 3–8 (approximately ages 8–13), and we take the following measures: Age-appropriate design: The Platform is designed with age-appropriate experiences, content, and interactions.

Protecting children is at the core of everything we do. Learnverse is designed for learners in Grades 3–8 (approximately ages 8–13), and we take the following measures: Parental access: Parents and guardians may review, request correction of, or request deletion of their child's personal data at any time by contacting us.

section: H2:9. Consent and Privacy by Default
sentence: Explicit consent: We obtain clear, affirmative consent before collecting personal data. Consent is requested at the point of data collection through clear, age-appropriate language. Users are informed of what data is being collected, why, and how it will be used before they provide consent.

section: H2:9. Consent and Privacy by Default
sentence: Privacy by default: Our Platform enforces privacy by default. This means: Data collection settings default to the minimum necessary.

section: H2:9. Consent and Privacy by Default
sentence: Privacy by default: Our Platform enforces privacy by default. This means: Optional data sharing features are turned off by default.

section: H2:9. Consent and Privacy by Default
sentence: Privacy by default: Our Platform enforces privacy by default. This means: Student profiles and learning data are private by default and visible only to the student, their assigned teacher(s), and school administrators.

section: H2:9. Consent and Privacy by Default
sentence: Privacy by default: Our Platform enforces privacy by default. This means: Analytics and research use only de-identified, aggregated data unless explicit additional consent is given.

section: H2:9. Consent and Privacy by Default
sentence: Withdrawing consent: Users (or parents/guardians on behalf of children) may withdraw consent at any time by contacting us at privacy@wizar.io. Withdrawal does not affect the lawfulness of processing conducted prior to withdrawal.

section: H2:10. Data Sharing and Third Parties
sentence: We do not sell personal or non-personal data to any third party.

section: H2:10. Data Sharing and Third Parties
sentence: We may share limited data with the following categories of service providers, solely to operate and improve the Platform:

section: H2:10. Data Sharing and Third Parties
sentence: | Service Provider Type | Purpose | Data Shared | Safeguards |

section: H2:10. Data Sharing and Third Parties
sentence: | Cloud hosting (e.g., AWS, Google Cloud) | Infrastructure and data storage | All Platform data (encrypted) | Data processing agreements; SOC 2 / ISO 27001 certified providers |

section: H2:10. Data Sharing and Third Parties
sentence: | Analytics (e.g., Google Analytics) | Platform performance monitoring | Anonymised usage data only | IP anonymisation enabled; no personal data shared |

section: H2:10. Data Sharing and Third Parties
sentence: | Communication (e.g., email service) | Service notifications | Email addresses | Data processing agreements; encrypted transmission |

section: H2:10. Data Sharing and Third Parties
sentence: All third-party service providers are bound by data processing agreements that require them to: Process data only on our instructions and for the specified purpose.

section: H2:10. Data Sharing and Third Parties
sentence: All third-party service providers are bound by data processing agreements that require them to: Implement appropriate security measures.

section: H2:10. Data Sharing and Third Parties
sentence: All third-party service providers are bound by data processing agreements that require them to: Not use data for their own purposes, including marketing.

section: H2:10. Data Sharing and Third Parties
sentence: All third-party service providers are bound by data processing agreements that require them to: Delete or return data upon termination of the relationship.

section: H2:10. Data Sharing and Third Parties
sentence: We may also disclose data if required by law, regulation, legal process, or governmental request.

section: H2:11. AI, Machine Learning, and Data
sentence: Learnverse utilises AI technologies to enhance the learning experience: Adaptive learning paths: AI analyses student progress data to personalise content sequencing and difficulty.

section: H2:11. AI, Machine Learning, and Data
sentence: Learnverse utilises AI technologies to enhance the learning experience: Generative AI features: Certain curriculum modules incorporate generative AI (e.g., for creative AI exercises like story writing or image generation). These features use third-party AI models (e.g., from providers like OpenAI) with the following safeguards:

section: H2:11. AI, Machine Learning, and Data
sentence: Learnverse utilises AI technologies to enhance the learning experience: No student personal data is sent to third-party AI providers. Interactions are anonymised.

section: H2:11. AI, Machine Learning, and Data
sentence: Learnverse utilises AI technologies to enhance the learning experience: AI guardrails and safety controls are in place to ensure age-appropriate outputs.

section: H2:11. AI, Machine Learning, and Data
sentence: Learnverse utilises AI technologies to enhance the learning experience: Machine-generated content is reviewed and sampled for accuracy and appropriateness.

section: H2:11. AI, Machine Learning, and Data
sentence: Learnverse utilises AI technologies to enhance the learning experience: Dataset origin: Our curriculum content was developed by our team of educators and subject matter experts. AI models used are third-party models accessed via API; we do not train proprietary models on student data.
We retain personal data only for as long as necessary to fulfil the purposes described in this policy:
| Data Type | Retention Period | After Retention |
| Active student accounts | Duration of enrolment or active use, plus 1 year | Deleted or anonymised |
| Teacher/admin accounts | Duration of active use, plus 2 years | Deleted or anonymised |
| Learning progress data | Duration of enrolment, plus 1 year to allow portfolio access | Anonymised for research or deleted |
| Support communications | 2 years from resolution | Deleted |
| Anonymised/aggregated data | Indefinitely (no individual can be identified) | N/A |
Users (or parents/guardians) may request earlier deletion at any time. Schools may request bulk deletion of student data when a partnership ends.
We implement industry-standard technical and organisational measures to protect data: Encryption: Data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (AES-256).
We implement industry-standard technical and organisational measures to protect data: Access controls: Role-based access ensures employees only access data necessary for their function.
We implement industry-standard technical and organisational measures to protect data: Infrastructure: Hosted on SOC 2-compliant cloud infrastructure with regular security audits.
We implement industry-standard technical and organisational measures to protect data: Vulnerability management: Regular patching, dependency scanning, and penetration testing.

We implement industry-standard technical and organisational measures to protect data: Incident response: Documented incident response procedures. In the event of a data breach that poses a risk to users, we will notify affected individuals and relevant authorities within 72 hours as required by applicable law.
We implement industry-standard technical and organisational measures to protect data: Employee training: All team members receive data protection and security awareness training.
Depending on your jurisdiction, you have the following rights regarding your personal data: Right of access: Request a copy of the personal data we hold about you (or your child).
Depending on your jurisdiction, you have the following rights regarding your personal data: Right to rectification: Request correction of inaccurate or incomplete data.
Depending on your jurisdiction, you have the following rights regarding your personal data: Right to erasure ("right to be forgotten"): Request deletion of your personal data.
Depending on your jurisdiction, you have the following rights regarding your personal data: Right to data portability: Request your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
Depending on your jurisdiction, you have the following rights regarding your personal data: Right to restrict processing: Request limitation of how we use your data.
Depending on your jurisdiction, you have the following rights regarding your personal data: Right to object: Object to processing based on legitimate interests.
Depending on your jurisdiction, you have the following rights regarding your personal data: Right to withdraw consent: Withdraw consent at any time where processing is based on consent.
Depending on your jurisdiction, you have the following rights regarding your personal data: Right to lodge a complaint: File a complaint with your local data protection authority.
To exercise any of these rights, contact us at privacy@wizar.io. We will respond within 30 days (or sooner if required by applicable law).

For users in the European Economic Area (EEA), United Kingdom, and Switzerland, we comply with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR): Legal bases for processing: We process personal data based on: (a) consent, (b) performance of a contract (providing the Service), (c) compliance with legal obligations, and (d) legitimate interests (Platform improvement), balanced against the individual's rights.

For users in the European Economic Area (EEA), United Kingdom, and Switzerland, we comply with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR): Data Protection Officer: Enquiries can be directed to privacy@wizar.io.

For users in the European Economic Area (EEA), United Kingdom, and Switzerland, we comply with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR): Supervisory authority: You have the right to lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority.

For users in the European Economic Area (EEA), United Kingdom, and Switzerland, we comply with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR): Cross-border transfers: See Section 18 (International Data Transfers).

For users in the United States, we comply with the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA): We obtain verifiable parental consent before collecting personal information from children under 13, except where schools provide consent under the school official exception.

For users in the United States, we comply with the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA): Parents can review their child's personal information, request its deletion, and refuse further collection.

For users in the United States, we comply with the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA): We do not condition a child's participation on providing more personal information than is reasonably necessary.

For users in the United States, we comply with the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA): We maintain reasonable procedures to protect the confidentiality, security, and integrity of children's personal information.

As a Canadian company, we comply with the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA) and applicable provincial privacy legislation: We obtain meaningful consent for the collection, use, and disclosure of personal information.

As a Canadian company, we comply with the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA) and applicable provincial privacy legislation: We collect personal information only for purposes that a reasonable person would consider appropriate.

As a Canadian company, we comply with the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA) and applicable provincial privacy legislation: Individuals may access their personal information and challenge its accuracy.

As a Canadian company, we comply with the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA) and applicable provincial privacy legislation: Personal information is protected by appropriate security safeguards.

As a Canadian company, we comply with the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA) and applicable provincial privacy legislation: Our privacy practices are open and transparent.

Wizar Learning operates in Canada and India. Your data may be processed in countries other than your country of residence. When we transfer data internationally, we ensure appropriate safeguards are in place: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission for transfers from the EEA.

Wizar Learning operates in Canada and India. Your data may be processed in countries other than your country of residence. When we transfer data internationally, we ensure appropriate safeguards are in place: Data processing agreements with all service providers that mandate equivalent protections.

Wizar Learning operates in Canada and India. Your data may be processed in countries other than your country of residence. When we transfer data internationally, we ensure appropriate safeguards are in place: Storage on infrastructure that complies with local data protection requirements.
We use cookies and similar technologies for essential Platform functionality: Essential cookies: Required for authentication, session management, and security. These cannot be disabled.
We use cookies and similar technologies for essential Platform functionality: Analytics cookies: Used to understand Platform usage and improve performance. These are opt-in and use anonymised data.
We use cookies and similar technologies for essential Platform functionality: No advertising cookies: We do not use cookies for advertising or cross-site tracking.
You can manage cookie preferences through your browser settings or through our cookie consent banner.

section: H2:20. Changes to This Privacy Policy
sentence: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make significant changes, we will: Notify you via email (for registered users) and through a prominent notice on the Platform.

section: H2:20. Changes to This Privacy Policy
sentence: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make significant changes, we will: Provide a summary of changes at the top of the updated policy.

section: H2:20. Changes to This Privacy Policy
sentence: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make significant changes, we will: Update the "Last updated" date at the top of this page.

section: H2:20. Changes to This Privacy Policy
sentence: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make significant changes, we will: Provide at least 30 days' notice before changes take effect for substantive modifications.

section: H2:20. Changes to This Privacy Policy
sentence: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make significant changes, we will: Where required by law, obtain renewed consent for any material changes to data processing.
If you have questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us:
Wizar Learning Inc.
Email: privacy@wizar.io
Website: www.wizar.io
Vancouver, BC, Canada
We aim to respond to all privacy enquiries within 30 days.
